title: Tesla Q4 2023 Earnings Report
content:
Tesla Inc. Q4 2023 Update - Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results

SAN FRANCISCO, January 25, 2024

TESLA Q4 AND FULL YEAR 2023 UPDATE

Q4 2023 Financial Results:
- Total Revenue: $25.2 billion (Q4), $81.5 billion (full year)
- Automotive Revenue: $22.9 billion (Q4), $74.8 billion (FY)
- Energy Storage and Services Revenue: $2.3 billion (Q4), $6.7 billion (FY)
- Gross Margin: 25.1% (Q4), 25.5% (FY)
- Operating Margin: 12.5% (Q4), 15.0% (FY)
- Net Income: $3.6 billion (Q4), $14.9 billion (FY)

Production and Deliveries:
- Total Vehicle Production: 1.81 million units (full year 2023)
- Total Vehicle Deliveries: 1.81 million units (full year 2023)
- Year-over-year growth: 38% production increase

By Model:
- Model Y/3: 1.23 million units shipped
- Model S/X: 0.381 million units shipped
- Cybertruck: 0.05 million units produced (ramp-up phase)

Operational Highlights:
- Significant production ramp at new Berlin and Austin plants
- Price optimization strategy maintained margins despite competitive pressure
- Energy storage deployment surged to 14.7 GWh (up 130% YoY)

Cypbertruck Launch:
- Production began October 2023 at Texas Gigafactory
- Initial production constrained as supply chain ramps
- Strong demand continues with 2 million+ reservations
- Ramping to meaningful volume levels during 2024

Capital Expenditure:
- Total Capex 2023: $8.2 billion
- Invested in: new manufacturing capacity, energy projects, AI/computing
- Planned facilities: Mexico Gigafactory, India Gigafactory

Full Year 2023 Summary:
Tesla achieved record production and delivery volumes while maintaining pricing power, despite increased competition in the EV market from traditional automakers. The company's gross margin remained healthy at 25.5%, reflecting production efficiency improvements and favorable energy storage growth.

Looking forward, Tesla expects accelerated growth through:
- Cybertruck ramp to volume
- New factory capacity utilization
- Energy business expansion
- AI/FSD development
- Next-generation vehicle development

title: Amazon Web Services (AWS) Financial Performance 2023
content:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) - 2023 Financial Performance and Strategic Update

AWS 2023 Full Year Results:
- Total Revenue: $90.8 billion (up 13% YoY)
- Operating Income: $21.5 billion (up 22% YoY)
- Operating Margin: 23.7% (up 180 basis points)

Revenue Breakdown by Service:
- Compute Services (EC2): $38.2 billion (42% of AWS revenue)
- Database and Analytics: $23.1 billion (25%)
- Storage Services: $15.4 billion (17%)
- Networking and Content Delivery: $8.9 billion (10%)
- Application Services and Other: $5.2 billion (6%)

Customer Metrics:
- Over 8 million active customers using AWS
- 30% of customers are enterprise Fortune 500 companies
- Customer base growing 30% YoY

Market Position:
- AWS estimated market share: 32% of global cloud infrastructure
- Largest cloud provider by revenue and market share
- Operating margins at 23.7%, highest among major cloud providers

AI and Machine Learning:
AWS accelerated AI adoption in 2023:
- SageMaker ML service revenue grew 45% YoY
- Bedrock foundation models launched (Claude, GPT models)
- Q model customization growing rapidly
- AI services revenue estimated at $4.2 billion (extrapolated growth rate 50% YoY)

Geographic Performance:
- US and International approximately equal revenue (~50/50)
- Europe growing 20% YoY
- Asia-Pacific growing 35% YoY
- Latin America growing 32% YoY

Competitive Dynamics:
- Microsoft Azure growing faster (29%) but from smaller base
- Google Cloud Platform growing 26% but in third position
- AWS maintaining 180 bps margin lead despite price competition

Capital Intensity:
- Capex investment in infrastructure: $29.3 billion
- Supporting data center buildout for AI
- Regional expansion to emerging markets

2024 Outlook:
AWS expects:
- Continued cloud adoption acceleration
- AI services proliferation
- Margin maintenance through efficiency gains
- Market share defense in competitive environment
